rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Narayanan, Manchanda, and Chintagunta (2005) as part of the core prior stream showing that multiple information sources affect new-drug diffusion. It then positions its own contribution as addressing what that stream did not combine: multiple-source learning with forward-looking physician behavior and expectations about future detailing. Although the citation appears in grouped discussion rather than detailed method borrowing, it plays a meaningful role in framing the research gap and contribution.
relation_description: The 2012 study builds on the Narayanan et al. (2005) learning-and-diffusion line by adding dynamic forward-looking physician decision making and strategic substitution between patient feedback and expected detailing. It extends that prior framing rather than merely listing it as background.
